messages.author: Vikram Patel
messages.text: 🚨 INCIDENT: Payment service is returning 500 errors for approximately 30% of transactions. Severity: P1. I'm declaring an incident and starting the investigation.

replies.author: Anushka Jain
replies.text: Looking into this now. I can see increased error rates starting at 14:15 UTC. The errors seem to be coming from the payment gateway integration layer.

replies.author: Deepa Krishnan
replies.text: Infrastructure side looks stable. CPU and memory are within normal ranges. Network latency is fine. This looks like an application-level issue.

replies.author: Anushka Jain
replies.text: Found the root cause! A database migration deployed at 14:10 UTC added a new NOT NULL column to the transactions table without a default value. Existing payment flows that don't populate this field are failing with constraint violations.

replies.author: Vikram Patel
replies.text: Good catch. Can we add a default value to fix this immediately?

replies.author: Anushka Jain
replies.text: Yes, deploying a hotfix now to ALTER the column and add DEFAULT 'standard'. Will also add a backfill for existing null rows.

replies.author: Anushka Jain
replies.text: Hotfix deployed. Error rates dropping. Back to normal levels as of 15:25 UTC. Total incident duration: ~70 minutes. Will write up a postmortem.

replies.author: Vikram Patel
replies.text: Great work team. Marking this as resolved. Postmortem review scheduled for Thursday. Action item: we need to add migration safety checks to our CI pipeline.

messages.author: Priya Menon
messages.text: FYI: Dashboard service latency has increased by 200ms after the latest deployment. Not a P1 but worth investigating.

replies.author: Arjun Nair
replies.text: I think this is related to the new analytics query we added. It's doing a full table scan instead of using the index. I'll optimize the query.

replies.author: Priya Menon
replies.text: Thanks Arjun. Please also check if the caching layer is working correctly for dashboard data.
